generatedSentence: Elara, a jealous amateur alchemist, tried to justify the constant inconvenience of her lab's perpetual vacancy, a former brewery now filled with ultrasonic emitters instead of flowers. She was a self-proclaimed freak of the future, believing her alchemy alone matters and her methods were always masterful. With rare candor, she would often disobey the regulations of the encroaching horde from the eastern sector, a society that practiced strict exogamy. Her sunflowers, growing randomly on a precarious scaffold, cast shadows on a hidden keyhole leading to a secret ring, often protected by a makeshift umbrella. She often gazed at distant galaxies, lamenting the obesity of consumerism. Despite a scattering of cheap trinkets, her lab truly lacked the close financial word of a waiver, plunging her into pandemonium. She kept a patchwork journal detailing her experiments with pineapple enzymes and arcane viscosity, a task she’d held since junior infancy. The janitor, an unarmed, obscure figure named Tantalus, often left flares adjacent to her labyrinthine workspace, unknowingly impeding her progress. His jokey nature and just a hint of a junkie's weariness gave his ugly, muscled ton a strange charm. Elara often felt her fate was fixed by unseen anchors, but she was entertained by the thought of becoming a grand jokester. She invents a plan to enact a playful vendetta, to upcycle the city's forgotten tech, even as a tickled sense of doom, like an approaching tornado, made her forlorn at midnight. With a cheeky grin, she imagined floating coconuts carrying messages to her sister, currently held in distant jails for minor infractions. Weirdly etched indigo symbols appeared in the internals of her machinery, a sign that her ancestral valor was being watched by vultures, but her current gimmicks were okayed by the city's corrupt arch-regulators. A sudden lightning strike, not legislation, shattered a cerebral cube, interrupting her whistling tune. The entire earth seemed to shudder, revealing an eternal source of power beneath her backyard, which the former sommelier, now a veterinarian, had always warned about. The itchy gamekeeper and his operators quickly dispatched a rocket to investigate, causing a slight hiccup in her harvest of energy. Joking with her ear to the ground, Elara knew it was time to restart her calculations and uncheck the warnings, ready for the next adventure.
panelSentences: Elara, a jealous amateur alchemist, tried to justify the constant inconvenience of her lab's perpetual vacancy, a former brewery now filled with ultrasonic emitters instead of flowers. She was a self-proclaimed freak of the future, believing her alchemy alone matters and her methods were always masterful. With rare candor, she would often disobey the regulations of the encroaching horde from the eastern sector, a society that practiced strict exogamy. Her sunflowers, growing randomly on a precarious scaffold, cast shadows on a hidden keyhole leading to a secret ring, often protected by a makeshift umbrella. His jokey nature and just a hint of a junkie's weariness gave his ugly, muscled ton a strange charm.

Elara often felt her fate was fixed by unseen anchors, but she was entertained by the thought of becoming a grand jokester. She invents a plan to enact a playful vendetta, to upcycle the city's forgotten tech, even as a tickled sense of doom, like an approaching tornado, made her forlorn at midnight. With a cheeky grin, she imagined floating coconuts carrying messages to her sister, currently held in distant jails for minor infractions. Weirdly etched indigo symbols appeared in the internals of her machinery, a sign that her ancestral valor was being watched by vultures, but her current gimmicks were okayed by the city's corrupt arch-regulators.

A sudden lightning strike, not legislation, shattered a cerebral cube, interrupting her whistling tune. The entire earth seemed to shudder, revealing an eternal source of power beneath her backyard, which the former sommelier, now a veterinarian, had always warned about. Joking with her ear to the ground, Elara knew it was time to restart her calculations and uncheck the warnings, ready for the next adventure.
rawPanelSentences: Elara, a jealous amateur alchemist, tried to justify the constant inconvenience of her lab's perpetual vacancy, a former brewery now filled with ultrasonic emitters instead of flowers. She was a self-proclaimed freak of the future, believing her alchemy alone matters and her methods were always masterful. With rare candor, she would often disobey the regulations of the encroaching horde from the eastern sector, a society that practiced strict exogamy. Her sunflowers, growing randomly on a precarious scaffold, cast shadows on a hidden keyhole leading to a secret ring, often protected by a makeshift umbrella. She often gazed at distant galaxies, lamenting the obesity of consumerism.

Despite a scattering of cheap trinkets, her lab truly lacked the close financial word of a waiver, plunging her into pandemonium. She kept a patchwork journal detailing her experiments with pineapple enzymes and arcane viscosity, a task she’d held since junior infancy. The janitor, an unarmed, obscure figure named Tantalus, often left flares adjacent to her labyrinthine workspace, unknowingly impeding her progress. His jokey nature and just a hint of a junkie's weariness gave his ugly, muscled ton a strange charm.

Elara often felt her fate was fixed by unseen anchors, but she was entertained by the thought of becoming a grand jokester. She invents a plan to enact a playful vendetta, to upcycle the city's forgotten tech, even as a tickled sense of doom, like an approaching tornado, made her forlorn at midnight. With a cheeky grin, she imagined floating coconuts carrying messages to her sister, currently held in distant jails for minor infractions. Weirdly etched indigo symbols appeared in the internals of her machinery, a sign that her ancestral valor was being watched by vultures, but her current gimmicks were okayed by the city's corrupt arch-regulators.

A sudden lightning strike, not legislation, shattered a cerebral cube, interrupting her whistling tune. The entire earth seemed to shudder, revealing an eternal source of power beneath her backyard, which the former sommelier, now a veterinarian, had always warned about. The itchy gamekeeper and his operators quickly dispatched a rocket to investigate, causing a slight hiccup in her harvest of energy. Joking with her ear to the ground, Elara knew it was time to restart her calculations and uncheck the warnings, ready for the next adventure.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A determined female alchemist with a shock of electric blue hair and glowing cybernetic goggles. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist Elara, looking through a keyhole, a massive glowing sunflower on a precarious scaffold in the background, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist Elara, unarmed, navigating a labyrinthine lab filled with glowing pineapple jars and archaic machinery, wide shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist Elara, with a cheeky grin, launching a coconut-shaped drone towards distant neon-lit jails under a midnight sky, dynamic angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist Elara, kneeling in her backyard, touching a newly revealed, pulsating cube of energy as a rocket ascends in the background, low angle.

definitions.definition: Suspecting rivalry in love; troubled by worries that one might have been replaced in someone's affections; suspicious of a lover's or spouse's fidelity.
Protective, zealously guarding, careful in the protection of something one has or appreciates.

definitions.definition: Envious; feeling resentful or angered toward someone for a perceived advantage or success, material or otherwise.
Suspecting, suspicious.
To provide an acceptable explanation for.
definitions.example: How can you justify spending so much money on clothes?

definitions.definition: To be a good, acceptable reason for; warrant.
definitions.example: Nothing can justify your rude behaviour last night.

definitions.definition: To arrange (text) on a page or a computer screen such that the left and right ends of all lines within paragraphs are aligned.

definitions.definition: To absolve, and declare to be free of blame or sin.
To give reasons for one’s actions; to make an argument to prove that one is in the right.

definitions.definition: To prove; to ratify; to confirm.
To show (a person) to have had a sufficient legal reason for an act that has been made the subject of a charge or accusation.
To qualify (oneself) as a surety by taking oath to the ownership of sufficient property.

A plant that bears flowers, especially a plant that is small and lacks wood.

definitions.definition: (usually with in) Of plants, a state of bearing blooms.

definitions.definition: (hypocoristic) The vulva, especially the labia majora.

definitions.definition: The best state of things; the prime.

definitions.definition: Flour.

definitions.definition: To decorate with pictures of flowers.
To reach a state of full development or achievement.
To froth; to ferment gently, as new beer.

Something that flows, such as a river.

definitions.definition: A hippie.

A person whose physique has grown far beyond the normal limits of muscular development; often a bodybuilder weighing more than 120 kilos (260 pounds).
An enthusiast, or person who has an obsession with, or extreme knowledge of, something.

definitions.definition: (sometimes affectionate) A very sexually perverse individual.

definitions.definition: A streak of colour; variegation.

Goodness in what is yet to come. Something to look forward to.

Only, simply, merely.
definitions.example: He calls it vermilion, but it's just red to me.

definitions.definition: (sentence adverb) Used to reduce the force of an imperative; simply.
definitions.example: Just follow the directions on the box.

definitions.definition: Used to convey a less serious or formal tone
definitions.example: I just called to say "hi".

definitions.definition: Used to show humility.
definitions.example: Lord, we just want to thank You and praise Your Name.

definitions.definition: (degree) absolutely, positively
definitions.example: It is just splendid!

definitions.definition: Moments ago, recently.
definitions.example: They just left, but you may leave a message at the desk.

definitions.definition: By a narrow margin; closely; nearly.
definitions.example: The fastball just missed my head!

definitions.definition: Exactly, precisely, perfectly.
definitions.example: He wants everything just right for the big day.

definitions.definition: Expressing dismay or discontent.
A narcotics addict, especially a heroin user.
(by extension) An enthusiast of something.
definitions.example: English people are travel junkies, but Americans hardly ever leave their state.

definitions.definition: Ugliness.
An ugly person or thing.
A shade for the face, projecting from a bonnet.
To make ugly (sometimes with up).
Displeasing to the eye; not aesthetically pleasing.
Displeasing to the ear or some other sense.
Offensive to one's sensibilities or morality.
definitions.example: He played an ugly trick on us.

definitions.definition: Ill-natured; crossgrained; quarrelsome.
definitions.example: an ugly temper; to feel ugly

definitions.definition: Unpleasant; disagreeable; likely to cause trouble or loss.
definitions.example: With all this competition, expect things to get ugly.

definitions.definition: A unit of weight (mass) equal to 2240 pounds (a long ton) or 2000 pounds (a short ton) or 1000 kilograms (a metric ton).
A unit of volume; register ton.

In refrigeration and air conditioning, a unit of thermal power defined as 12,000 BTU/h (about 3.514 kW or 3024 kcal/h), originally the rate of cooling provided by uniform isothermal melting of one short ton of ice per day at 32 °F (0 °C).
A large amount.
definitions.example: I've got tons of work to do.

definitions.definition: A speed of 100 mph.
One hundred pounds sterling.
One hundred runs.
(etc.) One hundred points scored.
A tool used to moor a vessel to the bottom of a sea or river to resist movement.
An iron device so shaped as to grip the bottom and hold a vessel at her berth by the chain or rope attached. (FM 55-501).
The combined anchoring gear (anchor, rode, bill/peak and fittings such as bitts, cat, and windlass.)
Representation of the nautical tool, used as a heraldic charge.

Any instrument serving a purpose like that of a ship's anchor, such as an arrangement of timber to hold a dam fast; a device to hold the end of a bridge cable etc.; or a device used in metalworking to hold the core of a mould in place.
A marked point in a document that can be the target of a hyperlink.
An anchorman or anchorwoman.
The final runner in a relay race.
A point that is touched by the draw hand or string when the bow is fully drawn and ready to shoot.
A superstore or other facility that serves as a focus to bring customers into an area.
That which gives stability or security.
A metal tie holding adjoining parts of a building together.
Carved work, somewhat resembling an anchor or arrowhead; part of the ornaments of certain mouldings. It is seen in the echinus, or egg-and-anchor (called also egg-and-dart, egg-and-tongue) ornament.
One of the anchor-shaped spicules of certain sponges.
One of the calcareous spinules of certain holothurians, as in species of Synapta.
The thirty-fifth Lenormand card.
An anchorite or anchoress.
To connect an object, especially a ship or a boat, to a fixed point.
To cast anchor; to come to anchor.
definitions.example: Our ship (or the captain) anchored in the stream.

definitions.definition: To stop; to fix or rest.
To provide emotional stability for a person in distress.
To perform as an anchorman or anchorwoman.
To be stuck; to be unable to move away from a position.
A measure of wine or spirit equal to 10 gallons; a barrel of this capacity.
To amuse (someone); to engage the attention of agreeably.
definitions.example: The motivational speaker not only instructed but also entertained the audience.

definitions.definition: To have someone over at one's home for a party or visit.
definitions.example: They enjoy entertaining a lot.

definitions.definition: To receive and take into consideration; to have a thought in mind.
definitions.example: The committee would like to entertain the idea of reducing the budget figures.

definitions.definition: To take or keep in one's service; to maintain; to support; to harbour; to keep.
To meet or encounter, as an enemy.
To lead on; to bring along; to introduce.
A person who tells jokes; a joker.
A person who plays practical jokes.
Jester, court jester.
To design a new process or mechanism.
definitions.example: After weeks of hard work, I invented a new way to alphabetize matchbooks.

definitions.definition: To create something fictional for a particular purpose.
definitions.example: I knew I had to invent an excuse, and quickly.

definitions.definition: To come upon; to find; to discover.
A bitter, destructive feud, normally between two families, clans or factions, in which each injury or slaying is revenged: a blood feud.
(often preceded by personal) A motivational grudge against a person or faction, which may or may not be reciprocated; the state of having it in for someone.
To convert (waste materials, etc.) into new materials or products of higher quality and greater functionality.
To promote into a more productive or useful role.
To touch repeatedly or stroke delicately in a manner which causes laughter, pleasure and twitching.
definitions.example: He tickled Nancy's tummy, and she started to giggle.

definitions.definition: To unexpectedly touch or stroke delicately in a manner which causes displeasure or withdrawal.
definitions.example: A stranger tickled Nancy's tummy, causing her to scream in fear.

definitions.definition: (of a body part) To feel as if the body part in question is being tickled.
definitions.example: My nose tickles, and I'm going to sneeze!

definitions.definition: To appeal to someone's taste, curiosity etc.
To cause delight or amusement in.
definitions.example: He was tickled to receive such a wonderful gift.

definitions.definition: To feel titillation.
To catch fish in the hand (usually in rivers or smaller streams) by manually stimulating the fins.
To be excited or heartened.
A violent windstorm characterized by a mobile, twisting, funnel-shaped cloud.
definitions.example: A tornado is a rotating column of air.

definitions.definition: To abandon, forsake.
A forlorn hope.
A member of a forlorn hope.
Abandoned, deserted, left behind.
Miserable, as when lonely after being abandoned.
Unlikely to succeed; hopeless.
The middle of the night: the sixth temporal hour, equidistant between sunset and sunrise.
12 o'clock at night exactly.
Utterly dark or black.
Impudent; impertinent; impertinently bold, often in a way that is regarded as endearing or amusing.
(of swimwear, underwear, etc.) tending to reveal the cheeks of the buttocks.
(Australian Aboriginal) Poisonous (of animals such as snakes), dangerous, cunning, violent, potent.
Indulged in.
A fruit of the coconut palm (not a true nut), Cocos nucifera, having a fibrous husk surrounding a large seed.
A hard-shelled seed of this fruit, having white flesh and a fluid-filled central cavity.
The edible white flesh of this fruit.
The coconut palm.
A Hispanic or dark-skinned person who acts “white” (Caucasian), alluding to the fact that a coconut is brown on the outside and white on the inside.
A black person who thinks or acts "white" (European).
A Pacific islander.
A female breast.
The human head (often used in cricket broadcasts when a ball hits or nearly hits a batsman on the head).
A doll.
A contrivance for stirring:
A tool with an indented head for shaping the head of a rivet.
In pile driving, a block interposed between the head of the pile and the ram of the driver.
A small truck with a single wide roller used for moving heavy beams, columns, etc., in bridge building.
A small truck without means of steering, to be slipped under a load.
A compact, narrow-gauge locomotive used for moving construction trains, switching, etc.
A specialized piece of film equipment resembling a little cart on which a camera is mounted.
A young woman, especially one who is frivolous or vapid.
A fashionable young woman, one who follows the latest music or clothing fashions.
A ball hit by a batsman such that it goes gently to a fielder for a simple catch.
A marker placed on the winning number by the dealer at roulette.
To hit a dolly.
To move (an object) using a dolly.
To wash (laundry) in a tub using the stirring device called a dolly.
To beat (red-hot metal) with a hammer.
To crush ore with a dolly.
In a weird manner

To cut into a surface with an acid or other corrosive substance in order to make a pattern. Best known as a technique for creating printing plates, but also used for decoration on metal, and, in modern industry, to make circuit boards.
To engrave a surface.
To make a lasting impression.
definitions.example: The memory of 9/11 is etched into my mind.

definitions.definition: To sketch; to delineate.
Cut or dug into the surface as by etching.
(of a muscle, body part or person) Toned and with a well-defined musculature.
A daughter of the same parents as another person; a female sibling.
definitions.example: My sister is always driving me crazy.

definitions.definition: A female member of a religious order; especially one devoted to more active service; (informally) a nun.
definitions.example: Michelle left behind her bank job and became a sister at the local convent.

definitions.definition: Any butterfly in the genus Adelpha, so named for the resemblance of the dark-colored wings to the black habit traditionally worn by nuns.
A senior or supervisory nurse, often in a hospital.
Any woman or girl with whom a bond is felt through common membership of a race, profession, religion or organization, such as feminism.
definitions.example: Connie was very close to her friend Judy and considered her to be her sister.

definitions.definition: (sometimes capitalized) A black woman.
A form of address to a woman.
definitions.example: What’s up, sister?

definitions.definition: A woman, in certain labour or socialist circles; also as a form of address.
definitions.example: Thank you, sister. I would like to thank the sister who just spoke.

definitions.definition: An entity that has a special or affectionate, non-hierarchical relationship with another.
definitions.example: sister city

definitions.definition: A node in a data structure that shares its parent with another node.
(usually attributively) Something in the same class.
definitions.example: sister facility

definitions.definition: To strengthen (a supporting beam) by fastening a second beam alongside it.
definitions.example: I’m trying to correct my sagging floor by sistering the joists.

definitions.definition: To be sister to; to resemble closely.
A place or institution for the confinement of persons held in lawful custody or detention, especially for minor offenses or with reference to some future judicial proceeding.
Confinement in a jail.
The condition created by the requirement that a horse claimed in a claiming race not be run at another track for some period of time (usually 30 days).
In dodgeball and related games, the area where players who have been struck by the ball are confined.
(FreeBSD) A kind of sandbox for running a guest operating system instance.
To imprison.
A purplish-blue colour
An indigo-colored dye obtained from certain plants (the indigo plant or woad), or a similar synthetic dye.
An indigo plant, such as from species in genera Indigofera, Amorpha (false indigo), Baptisia (wild indigo), and Psorothamnus and Dalea (indigo bush).
Having a deep purplish-blue colour
The internal workings of a mechanism or system that are normally hidden from view
Any of several carrion-eating birds of the families Accipitridae and Cathartidae.
A person who profits from the suffering of others.
definitions.example: Within ten minutes of the accident, the vultures appeared and were organizing lawsuits.

definitions.definition: To circle around one's target as if one were a vulture.
definitions.example: Rudy vultured when asking the girl out.

definitions.definition: Value; worth.
Strength of mind in regard to danger; that quality which enables a person to encounter danger with firmness
A trick or device used to attain some end.
definitions.example: The box had a gimmick to make the coin appear to vanish.

definitions.definition: A clever ploy or strategy.
definitions.example: The contest was a gimmick to get people to sign up for their mailing list.

definitions.definition: A gimmick capacitor.
A night out with one's friends.
To rig or set up with a trick or device.
definitions.example: The magician's box was gimmicked with a wire that made it appear to open on its own.

definitions.definition: To approve.
definitions.example: I don't want to OK this amount of money.

definitions.definition: To confirm by activating a button marked OK.
An inverted U shape.
An arch-shaped arrangement of trapezoidal stones, designed to redistribute downward force outward.
An architectural element having the shape of an arch
Any place covered by an arch; an archway.
definitions.example: to pass into the arch of a bridge

definitions.definition: An arc; a part of a curve.
A natural arch-shaped opening in a rock mass.
Curved part of the bottom of a foot.
To form into an arch shape
definitions.example: The cat arched its back

definitions.definition: To cover with an arch or arches.
Of, pertaining to, derived from, or possessed by, an ancestor or ancestors
definitions.example: an ancestral estate

definitions.definition: A flash of light produced by short-duration, high-voltage discharge of electricity within a cloud, between clouds, or between a cloud and the earth.
definitions.example: Although we did not see the lightning, we did hear the thunder.

definitions.definition: A discharge of this kind.
definitions.example: That tree was hit by lightning.

definitions.definition: Anything that moves very fast.
The act of making bright, or the state of being made bright; enlightenment; brightening, as of the mental powers.
To produce lightning.
Extremely fast or sudden; moving (as if) at the speed of lightning.
The act of legislating; preparation and enactment of laws; the laws enacted.
Law which has been enacted by legislature or other governing body
A regular polyhedron having six identical square faces.
Any object more or less in the form of a cube.
definitions.example: a stock cube

definitions.definition: The third power of a number, value, term or expression.
definitions.example: the cube of 2 is 8

definitions.definition: A data structure consisting of a three-dimensional array; a data cube
A Rubick's cube style puzzle, not necessarily in the shape of a cube
To raise to the third power; to determine the result of multiplying by itself twice.
definitions.example: Three cubed can be written as 33, and equals twenty-seven.

definitions.definition: To form into the shape of a cube.
To cut into cubes.
definitions.example: Cube the ham right after adding the curry to the rice.

definitions.definition: To use a Rubik's cube.
definitions.example: He likes to cube now and then.

definitions.definition: Of, or relating to the brain, cerebrum, or cerebral cortex.
Intellectual rather than emotional.
To make a shrill, high-pitched sound by forcing air through the mouth. To produce a whistling sound, restrictions to the flow of air are created using the teeth, tongue and lips.
definitions.example: Never whistle at a funeral.

definitions.definition: To make a similar sound by forcing air through a musical instrument or a pipe etc.
definitions.example: The stream train whistled as it passed by.

definitions.definition: To move in such a way as to create a whistling sound.
definitions.example: A bullet whistled past.

definitions.definition: To send, signal, or call by a whistle.
The third planet of the Solar System; the world upon which humans live.
The personification of the Earth or earth, as a fertile woman or goddess.
Soil.
definitions.example: This is good earth for growing potatoes.

definitions.definition: Any general rock-based material.
definitions.example: She sighed when the plane's wheels finally touched earth.

definitions.definition: The ground, land (as opposed to the sky or sea).
definitions.example: Birds are of the sky, not of the earth.

definitions.definition: A connection electrically to the earth ((US) ground); on equipment: a terminal connected in that manner.
The lair (as a hole on the ground) of an animal such as fox.
A region of the planet; a land or country.
Worldly things, as against spiritual ones.
The world of our current life (as opposed to heaven or an afterlife).
The people on the globe.
The human body.
The aforementioned soil- or rock-based material, considered one of the four or five classical elements.
Any of certain substances now known to be oxides of metal, which were distinguished by being infusible, and by insolubility in water.
To connect electrically to the earth.
definitions.example: That noise is because the amplifier is not properly earthed.

definitions.definition: To bury.
To burrow.
One who lives forever; an immortal.
Lasting forever; unending.
Existing outside time; as opposed to sempiternal, existing within time but everlastingly
Exceedingly great or bad; used as an intensifier.
definitions.example: some eternal villain

definitions.definition: The member of staff at a restaurant who keeps the wine cellar and advises the guests on a choice of wines; a wine steward / stewardess, a wine waiter / waitress / server.
definitions.example: The sommelier recommended the perfect wine, opened the bottle with panache, and served it into glasses.

definitions.definition: To act as a sommelier.
Having or creating an itch, causing a person or animal to tend to want to scratch.
A medical doctor who treats animals.
Veterinary
A yard to the rear of a house or similar residence.
A person's neighborhood, or an area nearby to a person's usual residence or place of work and where the person is likely to go.
An area nearby to a country or other jurisdiction's legal boundaries, particularly an area in which the country feels it has an interest.
A rocket engine.
A non-guided missile propelled by a rocket engine.
A vehicle propelled by a rocket engine.
A rocket propelled firework, a skyrocket
An ace (the playing card).
An angry communication (such as a letter or telegram) to a subordinate.
A blunt lance head used in jousting.
Something that shoots high in the air.
A stupid or crazy person.
To accelerate swiftly and powerfully
To fly vertically
To rise or soar rapidly
To carry something in a rocket
To attack something with rockets
A person employed to maintain the game for hunting and all associated materials and effects. Often shortened to keeper.
One who operates.
A telecommunications facilitator whose job is to establish temporary network connections.
A function or other mapping that carries variables defined on a domain into another variable or set of variables in a defined range.
The game of Chinese whispers.
A person who is adept at making deals or getting results, especially one who uses questionable methods.
A member of a military special operations unit.
The administrator of a channel or network on IRC.
A symbol that represents a construct in a programming language and differs from a normal function in its syntax.
A kind of expression that enters into an a-bar movement dependency and is said to bind a variable.
definitions.example: In the sentence "What did Bill say he wants to buy?", "what" is an operator, binding a phonetically empty variable.

definitions.definition: (grammar) A word that modifies a noun or describes a noun’s referent.
definitions.example: The words “big” and “heavy” are English adjectives.

definitions.definition: A dependent; an accessory.
To make an adjective of; to form or convert into an adjective.
(chiefly as a participle) To characterize with an adjective; to describe by using an adjective.
Incapable of independent function.
(grammar) Adjectival; pertaining to or functioning as an adjective.
Applying to methods of enforcement and rules of procedure.
(of a dye) Needing the use of a mordant to be made fast to that which is being dyed.
A person who gives lectures, especially as a profession.
A member of a university or college below the rank of assistant professor or reader.
A member of the Church of England clergy whose main task was to deliver sermons (lectures) in the afternoons and evenings.
The waging of war or armed conflict against an enemy.
Military operations of some particular kind e.g. guerrilla warfare.
To lead a military life; to carry on continual wars.
A spasm of the diaphragm, or the resulting sound.
definitions.example: There was a loud hiccup from the back of the room and the class erupted in laughter.

definitions.definition: (by extension) Any spasm or sudden change.
A minor setback.
definitions.example: There's been a slight hiccup in the processing of this quarter's results.

definitions.definition: To produce a hiccup; have the hiccups.
To say with a hiccup.
definitions.example: "I haven't touched a drop, officer," the suspect hiccupped.

definitions.definition: To produce an abortive sound like a hiccup.
definitions.example: The car engine hiccupped but wouldn't start.

definitions.definition: The third season of the year; autumn; fall.
definitions.example: Harvest is usually very damp and rainy.

definitions.definition: The season of gathering ripened crops; specifically, the time of reaping and gathering grain.
The process of gathering the ripened crop; harvesting.
The yield of harvesting, i.e., the gathered crops or fruits.
definitions.example: This year's cotton harvest was great but the corn harvest was disastrous.

definitions.definition: (by extension) The product or result of any exertion or course of action; reward or consequences.
A modern pagan ceremony held on or around the autumn equinox, which is in the harvesting season.
To bring in a harvest; reap; glean.
To be occupied bringing in a harvest
definitions.example: Harvesting is a stressing, thirsty occupation

definitions.definition: To win, achieve a gain.
definitions.example: The rising star harvested well-deserved acclaim, even an Oscar under 21

definitions.definition: The organ of hearing, consisting of the pinna, auditory canal, eardrum, malleus, incus, stapes and cochlea.
The external part of the organ of hearing, the auricle.
A police informant.
The sense of hearing; the perception of sounds; skill or good taste in listening to music.
definitions.example: a good ear for music

definitions.definition: The privilege of being kindly heard; favour; attention.
That which resembles in shape or position the ear of an animal; a prominence or projection on an object, usually for support or attachment; a lug; a handle.
definitions.example: the ears of a tub, skillet, or dish;   The ears of a boat are outside kneepieces near the bow.

definitions.definition: An acroterium.
A crossette.
To take in with the ears; to hear.
To hold by the ears.
The person, place or thing from which something (information, goods, etc.) comes or is acquired.
definitions.example: The accused refused to reveal the source of the illegal drugs she was selling.

definitions.definition: Spring; fountainhead; wellhead; any collection of water on or under the surface of the ground in which a stream originates.
definitions.example: The main sources of the Euphrates River are the Karasu and Murat Rivers.

definitions.definition: A reporter's informant.
Source code.
The name of one terminal of a field effect transistor (FET).
To obtain or procure: used especially of a business resource.
To find information about (a quotation)'s source (from which it comes): to find a citation for.
To do or say something for amusement rather than seriously.
definitions.example: I didn’t mean what I said — I was only joking.

definitions.definition: (intransitive, followed by with) To dupe in a friendly manner for amusement; to mess with, play with.
definitions.example: Relax, man, I'm just joking with you.

definitions.definition: To make merry with; to make jokes upon; to rally.
definitions.example: to joke a comrade

definitions.definition: The act of telling or engaging in jokes.
The act of starting something again.
definitions.example: After the restart of my browser, the problem went away.

definitions.definition: To start again.
definitions.example: All attempts to restart the engine failed.

definitions.definition: To reboot.
To remove a checkmark.
definitions.example: Uncheck the “save my password” box.

definitions.definition: (travel) To remove a previously checked-in passenger from the check-in system, allowing changes to the ticket.
